毒.origin: A plant part over 母; it pictures a harmful plant and means poison.

sentence.en: Snakes are poisonous.

毙.origin: 比 for the sound over 死 (death); it means to kill or die.

sentence.en: The hunter shot the wild boar dead.

毛.origin: A picture of hair or fur; it means hair, fur, or feathers.

sentence.en: I wipe my face with a towel.

毫.origin: 毛 (hair) with parts for the sound; it means a fine hair, tiny bit.

sentence.en: This line is ten millimeters long.

氏.origin: A picture of a person bowing; it means a clan or family name.

sentence.en: Please write down your surname.

氖.origin: 气 (gas) with 乃 for the sound; it names the gas neon.

sentence.en: The shop's neon lights are bright.

氟.origin: 气 (gas) with 弗 for the sound; it names the element fluorine.

sentence.en: There is fluorine in toothpaste.

氡.origin: 气 (gas) with 冬 for the sound; it names the gas radon.

sentence.en: There is some radon gas underground.

氢.origin: 气 (gas) with parts for the sound; it names hydrogen, the lightest gas.

sentence.en: The balloon is filled with hydrogen gas.

氦.origin: 气 (gas) with 亥 for the sound; it names the gas helium.

sentence.en: The helium balloon flew up into the sky.

氧.origin: 气 (gas) with 羊 for the sound; it names oxygen, the gas we breathe.

sentence.en: People cannot live without oxygen.

氨.origin: 气 (gas) with 安 for the sound; it names ammonia.

sentence.en: Ammonia has a sharp smell.

氩.origin: 气 (gas) with 亚 for the sound; it names the gas argon.

sentence.en: The light bulb is filled with argon gas.

氮.origin: 气 (gas) with 炎 for the sound; it names nitrogen.

sentence.en: There is a lot of nitrogen in the air.

氯.origin: 气 (gas) with 录 for the sound; it names the gas chlorine.

sentence.en: Chlorine gas is poisonous.

氽.origin: 人 (person) over 水 (water): floating on water; it means to float or boil quickly.

sentence.en: Mom quick-boiled a few meatballs.

汁.origin: 氵 (water) with 十 for the sound; it means juice or liquid.

sentence.en: I love to drink fruit juice.

汇.origin: Water 氵 gathering into a container 匚; it means to gather or remit.

sentence.en: Two rivers join together here.

汗.origin: 氵 (water) with 干 for the sound; it means sweat.

sentence.en: It's hot and I'm sweating.

汛.origin: 氵 (water) with 卂 for the sound; it means a flood or high water.

sentence.en: During flood season the river is high.

汝.origin: 氵 (water) with 女 for the sound; an old word meaning you.

sentence.en: The ancients used ru to mean you.

汞.origin: 工 over 水 (water); it names mercury, a liquid metal.

sentence.en: There is mercury in the thermometer.

池.origin: 氵 (water) with 也 for the sound; it means a pond or pool.

sentence.en: There are little fish in the pond.

汪.origin: 氵 (water) with 王 for the sound; it means a vast expanse of water; also a dog's bark.

sentence.en: The puppy goes woof woof.

汲.origin: 氵 (water) with 及 for the sound; it means to draw water.

sentence.en: He goes to the well to draw water.

沁.origin: 氵 (water) with 心 for the sound; it means to soak in or seep.

sentence.en: The flower's scent is refreshing.

沈.origin: 氵 (water) with 冘 for the sound; a surname, and an old form of 沉 (to sink).

sentence.en: He lives in Shenyang.

沏.origin: 氵 (water) with 切 for the sound; it means to brew with hot water.

sentence.en: Grandma brews tea for the guests.

沓.origin: 水 (water) over 日; it means repeated or piled up.

sentence.en: There is a stack of paper on the table.

沟.origin: 氵 (water) with 勾 for the sound; it means a ditch or channel.

sentence.en: The rainwater flows into the ditch.

沤.origin: 氵 (water) with 区 for the sound; it means to soak for a long time.

sentence.en: The farmer is making compost.

沪.origin: 氵 (water) with 户 for the sound; it is the short name for Shanghai.

sentence.en: He takes the train to Shanghai.

沫.origin: 氵 (water) with 末 for the sound; it means foam or froth.

sentence.en: The soap makes lots of bubbles.

沸.origin: 氵 (water) with 弗 for the sound; it means to boil.

sentence.en: The water is starting to boil.

沽.origin: 氵 (water) with 古 for the sound; it means to buy or sell.

sentence.en: He does not seek fame.

沾.origin: 氵 (water) with 占 for the sound; it means to get wet or to stick to.

word.en: to get wet, dip in water

sentence.en: My shoes got mud on them.

泄.origin: 氵 (water) with 世 for the sound; it means to leak or let out.

sentence.en: Don't lose heart, try again.

泅.origin: 氵 (water) with 囚 for the sound; it means to swim.

sentence.en: He swims across the river.

泉.origin: 白 over 水 (water): water flowing from the ground; it means a spring.

sentence.en: The spring water in the mountains is sweet.

泊.origin: 氵 (water) with 白 for the sound; it means to moor a boat; also calm.

sentence.en: The small boat is moored by the shore.

泓.origin: Wide 弘 water 氵 (弘 also gives the sound); a deep clear pool.

sentence.en: There is a pool of clear water below the hill.

泛.origin: 氵 (water) with 乏 for the sound; it means to float or be widespread.

sentence.en: His hobbies are very wide-ranging.

泡.origin: 氵 (water) with 包 for the sound; it means a bubble or to soak.

sentence.en: The children blow bubbles.

波.origin: 氵 (water) with 皮 for the sound; it means a wave.

sentence.en: There are big waves on the sea.

注.origin: 氵 (water) with 主 for the sound; it means to pour in or to focus.

sentence.en: Watch out for cars when crossing the road.

泪.origin: Water 氵 from the eye 目; it means tears.

sentence.en: She shed tears.

泰.origin: Water held back safely; it means peaceful, grand, or great.

sentence.en: Mount Tai is very tall.

泵.origin: 石 (stone) over 水 (water): a machine to push water; it means a pump.

sentence.en: The pump draws the water up.

泻.origin: 氵 (water) with 写 for the sound; it means to flow down fast or leak.

sentence.en: He ate something bad and had diarrhea.

泼.origin: 氵 (water) with 发 (to send forth); it means to splash or pour.

sentence.en: Little brother splashes water on the ground.
